description_long: This object is a shoulder-fired anti-tank weapon, commonly known as a rocket-propelled grenade launcher (RPG). It features a long cylindrical barrel with a flared muzzle at the front and a wide conical backblast shield at the rear. The main body is predominantly black, with a brown section near the rear, likely representing a wooden or polymer grip and shoulder rest. There is a prominent iron sight mounted on top, and a trigger assembly with a pistol grip beneath the barrel. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible mounting brackets and a simple, robust construction. This type of weapon is typically used in urban combat scenarios for engaging armored vehicles or fortified positions.
description: A black and brown shoulder-fired rocket launcher with a flared muzzle, conical backblast shield, and iron sights, designed for anti-armor use.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the circular backblast shield and the cylindrical body extending forward, with the iron sight visible on top.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the full length of the launcher, the flared muzzle, the brown grip, and the iron sight assembly.
description_view_2: The front view displays the wide, flared muzzle opening and the cylindrical barrel, with the iron sight silhouette above.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the conical backblast shield, brown grip, and the overall length of the launcher.
